assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response correctly states Regular channel clusters will automatically upgrade to next available version (likely 1.31+) and begin 2-4 weeks after EoS date

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response incorrectly states Extended channel provides 'up to 12 additional months' when it should be up to 24 months

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response mentions No channel clusters will auto-upgrade but doesn't specifically address the node pool force-upgrade behavior when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance windows for delaying upgrades but doesn't specifically mention the 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion mechanism or the 30-day limit

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response clearly recommends proactive manual upgrades and provides a detailed timeline for planning upgrades before auto-upgrade begins

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention Cloud Logging or EoS notifications as planning tools

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the deprecation insights dashboard or API compatibility checking

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention snowflaking, manually freezing versions, or the associated security and reliability risks
